name: Mongol Invasions of China (1211–1279)
detail: Genghis Khan's 1211 invasion of Jin Dynasty (northern China) and subsequent Mongol campaigns over 68 years destroyed major cities including Zhongdu (Beijing), killed millions, and fundamentally altered Chinese demographics. The Mongol conquest of the Southern Song completed China's subjugation under foreign rule for the first time.

name: Japanese Full-Scale Invasion (1937–1945)
detail: Following Marco Polo Bridge Incident (July 7, 1937), Japan launched full invasion. Occupied eastern China from Manchuria to Canton. Three Alls Policy ('Kill All, Burn All, Loot All') devastated northern China. Unit 731 conducted biological warfare experiments. Japan surrendered August 15, 1945 following US atomic bombings.

name: Eight-Nation Alliance Intervention (1900–1901)
detail: Britain, France, Germany, Russia, Japan, US, Austria-Hungary, and Italy deployed 20,000 troops to relieve Beijing legations besieged by Boxer rebels. Forces looted Forbidden City and imperial summer palace. Boxer Protocol imposed 450 million taels silver in reparations over 39 years.

name: Western Sanctions After Tiananmen (1989–present)
detail: US, EU, and other Western nations imposed economic sanctions and arms embargoes following the Tiananmen massacre. EU arms embargo remains in effect as of 2024. US Congress passed China-related sanctions; bilateral relations entered multi-year freeze before engagement resumed. China expelled from G7's predecessor G8 process.

name: US-China Trade War (2018–present)
detail: Trump administration imposed tariffs on $360 billion in Chinese goods (Section 301 action, 2018) citing IP theft and trade imbalances. China retaliated with tariffs on $110 billion in US goods. Phase 1 deal signed January 2020. Biden maintained most Trump tariffs; added new restrictions on semiconductors (October 2022 chip export controls). Trump 2.0 tariffs reached 145% on Chinese goods by April 2025.
